response.justification: Strong geospatial domain knowledge through GISCollective and prior navigation work, plus proven ability to master new technical stacks and deep software craftsmanship. However, C++ expertise dates to 2014-2015 with ten years of primary TypeScript/Node.js development since. The role requires modern C++ as primary technology, creating a significant gap for a mid-senior individual contributor. Could succeed if C++ learning velocity is proven, but represents material ramp-up risk.
strengths: Deep geospatial and navigation domain expertise through GISCollective platform and Green Map web GIS application
Demonstrated ability to rapidly master diverse technical stacks across languages, platforms, and domains
Strong testing and software craftsmanship background with TDD, CI/CD, infrastructure experience, and multiple workshops taught
concerns: C++ expertise is from 2014-2015; past decade focused on TypeScript/Node.js, not modern C++ development
Recent roles emphasize application-layer development rather than low-level systems, data pipelines, or performance optimization work
No Rust experience; limited evidence of building high-performance, low-latency distributed systems beyond one Dunnhumby micro-services role
